headline: Cabotage Exemption Saves Sabah Importers RM800 Million
context: The 2024 cabotage policy exemption for Sabah and Sarawak reduced shipping costs by 25-40% for essential goods.

cards.big: Shipping costs dropped 25-40% after 2024 cabotage exemption
cards.sub: Sabah businesses saved an estimated RM800 million in the first full year

cards.big: Container from Port Klang to KK now costs RM2,800 versus RM4,500 before
cards.sub: Foreign vessels can now compete on East Malaysia routes for the first time

cards.big: Malaysian Shipowners Association lost 35% of Borneo route contracts
cards.sub: Local shipping firms lobbied Parliament to reverse the exemption in late 2025

cards.big: Consumer prices in Sabah fell 8% for imported goods in 12 months
cards.sub: Rural areas still face last-mile delivery costs that offset shipping savings

cards.big: Cabotage reform tests whether monopoly protection or consumer welfare drives policy
Cabotage reform saved RM800 million — proof that monopoly costs fall heaviest on the periphery.
